---
title: "EnrichmentAnalysis"
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-15"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,   # show code by default
  results = 'hide', # hide printed output by default
  message = FALSE,  # hide package startup messages
  warning = FALSE   # hide warnings
)
```

### 2) Read functional info from InterProScan to get a clean list of GO terms

```{r build_custom_orgdb_interpro_eggnog}
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build custom OrgDb for Stylophora pistillata
# using InterProScan + eggNOG-mapper GO annotations
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(rtracklayer)
library(ontologyIndex)
library(AnnotationForge)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0. Input files and package metadata
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

gff_file      <- "complete.genomic.gff"
interpro_file <- "complete.genomic.faa.pstillata.v052.interproscan.results.tsv"
eggnog_file   <- "eggnog_results/complete.proteins.emapper.annotations"
go_obo_file   <- "go.obo"

pkg_version <- "0.6.0"
out_dir     <- "."
tax_id      <- "50429"
genus       <- "Stylophora"
species     <- "pistillata2"

orgdb_pkg_name <- "org.Spistillata2.eg.db"

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Import GFF and flatten list columns from rtracklayer
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

flatten_gff_col <- function(x) {
  if (is.list(x)) {
    vapply(
      x,
      function(y) {
        if (length(y) == 0 || all(is.na(y))) {
          NA_character_
        } else {
          paste(as.character(y), collapse = ",")
        }
      },
      character(1)
    )
  } else {
    as.character(x)
  }
}

gr <- rtracklayer::import(gff_file)

df <- as_tibble(gr) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(seqid = as.character(seqnames))

needed_cols <- c(
  "ID", "Parent", "gene", "Name", "product", "description",
  "locus_tag", "protein_id", "orig_protein_id", "orig_transcript_id"
)

for (cc in needed_cols) {
  if (!cc %in% names(df)) {
    df[[cc]] <- NA_character_
  }
}

df <- df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      dplyr::all_of(needed_cols),
      flatten_gff_col
    )
  )

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Build gene-level annotation tables
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GID      = stable locus_tag, e.g. egapxtmp_004582
# SYMBOL   = gene symbol if available, otherwise Name/locus_tag
# GENENAME = readable description/product/name fallback

gene_features <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(type == "gene") %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    gene_feature_id = ID,
    GID = locus_tag,
    SYMBOL = dplyr::coalesce(
      dplyr::na_if(gene, ""),
      dplyr::na_if(Name, ""),
      locus_tag
    ),
    GENENAME = dplyr::coalesce(
      dplyr::na_if(description, ""),
      dplyr::na_if(product, ""),
      dplyr::na_if(Name, ""),
      dplyr::na_if(gene, ""),
      locus_tag
    ),
    CHROMOSOME = seqid
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_feature_id), gene_feature_id != "") %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GID), GID != "") %>%
  dplyr::distinct(gene_feature_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

gene_info <- gene_features %>%
  dplyr::select(GID, SYMBOL, GENENAME) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(GID, .keep_all = TRUE)

chromosome <- gene_features %>%
  dplyr::select(GID, CHROMOSOME) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GID), GID != "") %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(CHROMOSOME), CHROMOSOME != "") %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

cat("Genes in GFF gene features:", dplyr::n_distinct(gene_features$GID), "\n")
cat("Rows in gene_info:", nrow(gene_info), "\n")
cat("Rows in chromosome:", nrow(chromosome), "\n")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Build mRNA -> gene map
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

mrna_to_gene <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(type == "mRNA") %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    mrna_id = ID,
    gene_feature_id = Parent,
    mrna_locus_tag = locus_tag,
    mrna_gene_symbol = gene,
    orig_protein_id = orig_protein_id,
    orig_transcript_id = orig_transcript_id,
    product = product
  ) %>%
  tidyr::separate_rows(gene_feature_id, sep = ",") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mrna_id = stringr::str_trim(mrna_id),
    gene_feature_id = stringr::str_trim(gene_feature_id)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    gene_features %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_feature_id, GID, SYMBOL, GENENAME),
    by = "gene_feature_id"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(mrna_id), mrna_id != "") %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GID), GID != "") %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

cat("mRNAs mapped to genes:", dplyr::n_distinct(mrna_to_gene$mrna_id), "\n")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Build clean protein accession -> gene map
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# InterProScan and eggNOG protein queries should match values like:
# gnl|WGS:ZZZZ|egapxtmp_004582-P1
#
# In this GFF, those accessions are stored on CDS rows as:
# Name, protein_id, and/or orig_protein_id.

protein_to_gene_long <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(type == "CDS") %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    mrna_id = Parent,
    Name = Name,
    protein_id = protein_id,
    orig_protein_id = orig_protein_id
  ) %>%
  tidyr::separate_rows(mrna_id, sep = ",") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(mrna_id = stringr::str_trim(mrna_id)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    mrna_to_gene %>%
      dplyr::select(mrna_id, gene_feature_id, GID, SYMBOL, GENENAME),
    by = "mrna_id"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Name, protein_id, orig_protein_id),
    names_to = "id_source",
    values_to = "protein_accession"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(protein_accession = stringr::str_trim(protein_accession)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(protein_accession), protein_accession != "") %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GID), GID != "") %>%
  dplyr::distinct(protein_accession, GID, SYMBOL, GENENAME, id_source)

ambiguous_protein_map <- protein_to_gene_long %>%
  dplyr::distinct(protein_accession, GID) %>%
  dplyr::count(protein_accession, name = "n_gid") %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_gid > 1)

cat("Protein accessions mapping to multiple genes:", nrow(ambiguous_protein_map), "\n")

if (nrow(ambiguous_protein_map) > 0) {
  protein_to_gene_long %>%
    dplyr::semi_join(ambiguous_protein_map, by = "protein_accession") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(protein_accession, GID) %>%
    print(n = 50)
}

protein_to_gene <- protein_to_gene_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    id_source_priority = dplyr::case_when(
      id_source == "protein_id" ~ 1L,
      id_source == "orig_protein_id" ~ 2L,
      id_source == "Name" ~ 3L,
      TRUE ~ 99L
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(protein_accession, id_source_priority) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(protein_accession, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    protein_accession,
    GID,
    SYMBOL,
    GENENAME,
    id_source
  )

cat("Unique protein accessions in GFF map:", dplyr::n_distinct(protein_to_gene$protein_accession), "\n")

# Optional known-example check
protein_to_gene %>%
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(protein_accession, "egapxtmp_004582")) %>%
  print(n = 20)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Read InterProScan GO annotations and map protein -> gene
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

IPS_header <- c(
  "Protein_accession", "Sequence_MD5", "Sequence_length", "Analysis",
  "Signature_accession", "Signature_description", "Start", "Stop", "Score",
  "Status", "Date", "InterPro_accession", "InterPro_description",
  "GO_terms", "Pathways"
)

interpro_raw <- read.delim(
  interpro_file,
  header = FALSE,
  sep = "\t",
  col.names = IPS_header,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

interpro_go_protein <- interpro_raw %>%
  dplyr::select(Protein_accession, GO_terms) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_terms), GO_terms != "-") %>%
  tidyr::separate_rows(GO_terms, sep = "\\|") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Protein_accession = stringr::str_trim(Protein_accession),
    GO = stringr::str_remove(GO_terms, "\\(.*\\)"),
    GO = stringr::str_trim(GO)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(GO, "^GO:\\d{7}$")) %>%
  dplyr::select(Protein_accession, GO) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

interpro_go <- interpro_go_protein %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    protein_to_gene,
    by = c("Protein_accession" = "protein_accession")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    fallback_GID = Protein_accession %>%
      stringr::str_remove("^.*\\|") %>%
      stringr::str_remove("-P[0-9]+$"),
    GID_final = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(GID) ~ GID,
      fallback_GID %in% gene_info$GID ~ fallback_GID,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    map_method = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(GID) ~ "gff_exact",
      is.na(GID) & fallback_GID %in% gene_info$GID ~ "regex_fallback",
      TRUE ~ "unmapped"
    )
  )

cat("\n--- InterProScan protein-to-gene mapping ---\n")

interpro_go %>%
  dplyr::distinct(Protein_accession, GID_final, map_method) %>%
  dplyr::count(map_method) %>%
  print()

interpro_go <- interpro_go %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GID_final)) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    GID = GID_final,
    GO = GO,
    EVIDENCE = "IEA",
    SOURCE = "InterProScan"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Read eggNOG-mapper GO annotations and map protein -> gene
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# eggNOG annotations file:
#   query column = #query
#   GO column    = GOs
#   GO terms are comma-separated.

eggnog_raw <- readr::read_tsv(
  eggnog_file,
  comment = "##",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

if ("#query" %in% names(eggnog_raw)) {
  eggnog_raw <- eggnog_raw %>%
    dplyr::rename(query = `#query`)
}

stopifnot("query" %in% names(eggnog_raw))
stopifnot("GOs" %in% names(eggnog_raw))

eggnog_go_protein <- eggnog_raw %>%
  dplyr::select(query, GOs) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GOs), GOs != "-", GOs != "") %>%
  tidyr::separate_rows(GOs, sep = ",") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    query = stringr::str_trim(query),
    GO = stringr::str_trim(GOs)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(GO, "^GO:\\d{7}$")) %>%
  dplyr::select(query, GO) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

eggnog_go <- eggnog_go_protein %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    protein_to_gene,
    by = c("query" = "protein_accession")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    fallback_GID = query %>%
      stringr::str_remove("^.*\\|") %>%
      stringr::str_remove("-P[0-9]+$"),
    GID_final = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(GID) ~ GID,
      fallback_GID %in% gene_info$GID ~ fallback_GID,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    map_method = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(GID) ~ "gff_exact",
      is.na(GID) & fallback_GID %in% gene_info$GID ~ "regex_fallback",
      TRUE ~ "unmapped"
    )
  )

cat("\n--- eggNOG protein-to-gene mapping ---\n")

eggnog_go %>%
  dplyr::distinct(query, GID_final, map_method) %>%
  dplyr::count(map_method) %>%
  print()

eggnog_go <- eggnog_go %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GID_final)) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    GID = GID_final,
    GO = GO,
    EVIDENCE = "IEA",
    SOURCE = "eggNOG-mapper"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Brief comparison: InterProScan vs eggNOG
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- GO source comparison: gene-level coverage ---\n")

ips_genes <- unique(interpro_go$GID)
egg_genes <- unique(eggnog_go$GID)

gene_source_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  category = c(
    "InterProScan genes with GO",
    "eggNOG genes with GO",
    "Shared genes with GO",
    "InterProScan-only genes with GO",
    "eggNOG-only genes with GO",
    "Combined genes with GO",
    "Total genes in gene_info"
  ),
  n_genes = c(
    length(ips_genes),
    length(egg_genes),
    length(intersect(ips_genes, egg_genes)),
    length(setdiff(ips_genes, egg_genes)),
    length(setdiff(egg_genes, ips_genes)),
    length(union(ips_genes, egg_genes)),
    dplyr::n_distinct(gene_info$GID)
  )
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    pct_of_gene_info = round(
      100 * n_genes / dplyr::n_distinct(gene_info$GID),
      2
    )
  )

print(gene_source_summary)

cat("\n--- GO source comparison: exact GID-GO pair overlap ---\n")

ips_pairs <- interpro_go %>%
  dplyr::select(GID, GO) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

egg_pairs <- eggnog_go %>%
  dplyr::select(GID, GO) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

go_pair_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  category = c(
    "InterProScan GID-GO pairs",
    "eggNOG GID-GO pairs",
    "Shared exact GID-GO pairs",
    "InterProScan-only GID-GO pairs",
    "eggNOG-only GID-GO pairs",
    "Combined exact GID-GO pairs"
  ),
  n_pairs = c(
    nrow(ips_pairs),
    nrow(egg_pairs),
    nrow(dplyr::inner_join(ips_pairs, egg_pairs, by = c("GID", "GO"))),
    nrow(dplyr::anti_join(ips_pairs, egg_pairs, by = c("GID", "GO"))),
    nrow(dplyr::anti_join(egg_pairs, ips_pairs, by = c("GID", "GO"))),
    nrow(dplyr::distinct(dplyr::bind_rows(ips_pairs, egg_pairs)))
  )
)

print(go_pair_summary)

cat("\n--- GO source comparison: GO-term-level overlap ---\n")

ips_terms <- unique(interpro_go$GO)
egg_terms <- unique(eggnog_go$GO)

go_term_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  category = c(
    "InterProScan unique GO terms",
    "eggNOG unique GO terms",
    "Shared GO terms",
    "InterProScan-only GO terms",
    "eggNOG-only GO terms",
    "Combined GO terms"
  ),
  n_terms = c(
    length(ips_terms),
    length(egg_terms),
    length(intersect(ips_terms, egg_terms)),
    length(setdiff(ips_terms, egg_terms)),
    length(setdiff(egg_terms, ips_terms)),
    length(union(ips_terms, egg_terms))
  )
)

print(go_term_summary)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Combine GO annotations and keep provenance
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

combined_go_with_source <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  interpro_go,
  eggnog_go
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(GID %in% gene_info$GID) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

readr::write_tsv(
  combined_go_with_source,
  "combined_GO_with_source.tsv"
)

cat("\nCombined GO rows with source:", nrow(combined_go_with_source), "\n")
cat("Combined genes with GO:", dplyr::n_distinct(combined_go_with_source$GID), "\n")
cat("Combined GO terms:", dplyr::n_distinct(combined_go_with_source$GO), "\n")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Load GO ontology and validate terms
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

if (!file.exists(go_obo_file)) {
  go_obo_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dadrasarmin/enrichment_analysis_for_non_model_organism/refs/heads/main/go.obo"
  download.file(
    url = go_obo_url,
    destfile = go_obo_file,
    mode = "wb",
    quiet = FALSE
  )
}

ontology <- ontologyIndex::get_ontology(
  file = go_obo_file,
  propagate_relationships = "is_a",
  extract_tags = "everything",
  merge_equivalent_terms = TRUE
)

valid_go_terms <- names(ontology$name)

invalid_go_terms <- setdiff(unique(combined_go_with_source$GO), valid_go_terms)

cat("\nInvalid GO terms:", length(invalid_go_terms), "\n")

if (length(invalid_go_terms) > 0) {
  print(head(invalid_go_terms, 50))
}

combined_go_valid <- combined_go_with_source %>%
  dplyr::filter(GO %in% valid_go_terms)

term2name_GO <- combined_go_valid %>%
  dplyr::mutate(name = ontology$name[GO]) %>%
  dplyr::select(term = GO, name) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  tidyr::drop_na() %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    !stringr::str_detect(
      name,
      stringr::regex("obsolete", ignore_case = TRUE)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(name)

combined_go_valid <- combined_go_valid %>%
  dplyr::filter(GO %in% term2name_GO$term) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

term2gene_GO <- combined_go_valid %>%
  dplyr::select(term = GO, gene = GID) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

readr::write_tsv(term2gene_GO, "term2gene_GO.tsv")
readr::write_tsv(term2name_GO, "term2name_GO.tsv")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Build GO table for AnnotationForge
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

go <- combined_go_valid %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    GID = GID,
    GO = GO,
    EVIDENCE = EVIDENCE
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(GID %in% gene_info$GID) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

stopifnot(nrow(go) > 0)
stopifnot(all(go$GID %in% gene_info$GID))
stopifnot(all(stringr::str_detect(go$GO, "^GO:\\d{7}$")))

cat("\n--- Final combined OrgDb input summary ---\n")
cat("gene_info rows:", nrow(gene_info), "\n")
cat("chromosome rows:", nrow(chromosome), "\n")
cat("GO rows:", nrow(go), "\n")
cat("Genes with GO:", dplyr::n_distinct(go$GID), "\n")
cat("GO terms:", dplyr::n_distinct(go$GO), "\n")
cat(
  "Percent genes with GO:",
  round(100 * dplyr::n_distinct(go$GID) / dplyr::n_distinct(gene_info$GID), 2),
  "%\n"
)

cat("\n--- GO terms per gene ---\n")

go %>%
  dplyr::count(GID, name = "n_go_terms") %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    min = min(n_go_terms),
    median = median(n_go_terms),
    mean = mean(n_go_terms),
    max = max(n_go_terms)
  ) %>%
  print()

cat("\n--- Genes per GO term, top 30 ---\n")

go %>%
  dplyr::count(GO, name = "n_genes") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    term2name_GO %>%
      dplyr::rename(GO = term),
    by = "GO"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_genes)) %>%
  print(n = 30)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. Build and install OrgDb package
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

AnnotationForge::makeOrgPackage(
  gene_info  = gene_info,
  chromosome = chromosome,
  go         = go,
  version    = pkg_version,
  maintainer = "Andreas Bachler <Andy.Bachler@csiro.au>",
  author     = "Andreas Bachler <Andy.Bachler@csiro.au>",
  outputDir  = out_dir,
  tax_id     = tax_id,
  genus      = genus,
  species    = species,
  goTable    = "go"
)

install.packages(orgdb_pkg_name, repos = NULL, type = "source")
```

```{r}
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test installed OrgDb
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

library(AnnotationDbi)

library(org.Spistillata2.eg.db)

orgdb <- org.Spistillata2.eg.db

cat("\n--- OrgDb keytypes ---\n")
print(AnnotationDbi::keytypes(orgdb))

cat("\n--- OrgDb columns ---\n")
print(AnnotationDbi::columns(orgdb))

# Pull a few test genes from your built gene_info table
test_keys <- head(gene_info$GID, 20)

cat("\n--- Test select from OrgDb ---\n")

orgdb_test <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  orgdb,
  keys = test_keys,
  keytype = "GID",
  columns = c("GID", "SYMBOL", "GENENAME", "GO", "EVIDENCE")
) %>%
  tibble::as_tibble()

print(orgdb_test, n = 50)

# Check whether OrgDb GO rows match the input go table

org_go <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  orgdb,
  keys = AnnotationDbi::keys(orgdb, keytype = "GID"),
  keytype = "GID",
  columns = c("GID", "GO", "EVIDENCE")
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO)) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(GID, GO, EVIDENCE)

input_go <- go %>%
  dplyr::distinct(GID, GO, EVIDENCE)

missing_from_orgdb <- input_go %>%
  dplyr::anti_join(org_go, by = c("GID", "GO", "EVIDENCE"))

extra_in_orgdb <- org_go %>%
  dplyr::anti_join(input_go, by = c("GID", "GO", "EVIDENCE"))

cat("\n--- OrgDb round-trip check ---\n")
cat("Input GO rows:", nrow(input_go), "\n")
cat("OrgDb GO rows:", nrow(org_go), "\n")
cat("Input rows missing from OrgDb:", nrow(missing_from_orgdb), "\n")
cat("Extra OrgDb rows:", nrow(extra_in_orgdb), "\n")

### Generally Obsolete terms
if (nrow(missing_from_orgdb) > 0) {
  print(head(missing_from_orgdb, 50))
}
if (nrow(extra_in_orgdb) > 0) {
  print(head(extra_in_orgdb, 50))
}
```

### 3) Quick check with 'heatshock proteins'
```{r}
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Heat-shock positive-control test using the installed OrgDb package
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This tests the actual generated org.Spistillata2.eg.db package,
# not just the pre-build TERM2GENE and TERM2NAME tables.
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(AnnotationDbi)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Spistillata2.eg.db)

orgdb <- org.Spistillata2.eg.db

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Confirm OrgDb structure
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- OrgDb keytypes ---\n")
print(AnnotationDbi::keytypes(orgdb))

cat("\n--- OrgDb columns ---\n")
print(AnnotationDbi::columns(orgdb))

stopifnot("GID" %in% AnnotationDbi::keytypes(orgdb))
stopifnot(all(c("GID", "SYMBOL", "GENENAME", "GO", "EVIDENCE") %in% AnnotationDbi::columns(orgdb)))

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Get all genes from the installed OrgDb
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

all_orgdb_genes <- AnnotationDbi::keys(
  orgdb,
  keytype = "GID"
) %>%
  unique()

cat("Total genes in installed OrgDb:", length(all_orgdb_genes), "\n")

# Pull gene-level annotation from the installed OrgDb.
# This deliberately uses the installed database, not gene_info.

orgdb_gene_info <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  orgdb,
  keys = all_orgdb_genes,
  keytype = "GID",
  columns = c("GID", "SYMBOL", "GENENAME")
) %>%
  tibble::as_tibble() %>%
  dplyr::distinct(GID, SYMBOL, GENENAME)

cat("Rows in OrgDb gene annotation table:", nrow(orgdb_gene_info), "\n")
cat("Unique GIDs in OrgDb gene annotation table:", dplyr::n_distinct(orgdb_gene_info$GID), "\n")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Pull heat-shock genes from the installed OrgDb annotation
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This reproduces your logic, but uses GENENAME from the installed OrgDb.

heat_shock_genes_list <- orgdb_gene_info %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    stringr::str_detect(
      GENENAME,
      stringr::regex("heat shock", ignore_case = TRUE)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(GID) %>%
  dplyr::pull() %>%
  unique()

cat("Unique heat-shock genes found in installed OrgDb:", length(heat_shock_genes_list), "\n")

print(heat_shock_genes_list)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Check whether those genes have GO terms in the installed OrgDb
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

heatshock_orgdb_go <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  orgdb,
  keys = heat_shock_genes_list,
  keytype = "GID",
  columns = c("GID", "SYMBOL", "GENENAME", "GO", "EVIDENCE")
) %>%
  tibble::as_tibble()

heatshock_check <- heatshock_orgdb_go %>%
  dplyr::group_by(GID, SYMBOL, GENENAME) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_go_terms = dplyr::n_distinct(GO[!is.na(GO)]),
    has_go = n_go_terms > 0,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_go_terms), GID)

cat("\n--- Heat-shock genes and GO coverage in installed OrgDb ---\n")
print(heatshock_check, n = Inf)

heatshock_summary <- heatshock_check %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_heatshock_genes = dplyr::n(),
    n_with_go = sum(has_go),
    n_without_go = sum(!has_go),
    pct_with_go = round(100 * mean(has_go), 2)
  )

cat("\n--- Heat-shock GO coverage summary ---\n")
print(heatshock_summary)

# Only genes with GO terms can contribute to enrichGO.
heatshock_genes_valid <- heatshock_check %>%
  dplyr::filter(has_go) %>%
  dplyr::select(GID) %>%
  dplyr::pull() %>%
  unique()

cat("Heat-shock genes usable for enrichGO:", length(heatshock_genes_valid), "\n")

if (length(heatshock_genes_valid) < 3) {
  stop("Fewer than 3 heat-shock genes have GO terms in the installed OrgDb. Enrichment is not meaningful.")
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Run GO enrichment using the installed OrgDb
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This is the key test:
# clusterProfiler queries org.Spistillata2.eg.db directly.

ego_heatshock_all <- clusterProfiler::enrichGO(
  gene = heatshock_genes_valid,
  universe = all_orgdb_genes,
  OrgDb = orgdb,
  keyType = "GID",
  ont = "ALL",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 1,
  qvalueCutoff = 1,
  minGSSize = 2,
  maxGSSize = 500,
  readable = FALSE,
  pool = FALSE
)

heatshock_results_all <- as.data.frame(ego_heatshock_all) %>%
  tibble::as_tibble()

cat("\n--- enrichGO results using installed OrgDb, ontology = ALL ---\n")
cat("GO terms returned:", nrow(heatshock_results_all), "\n")

heatshock_results_all %>%
  dplyr::select(
    ONTOLOGY,
    ID,
    Description,
    GeneRatio,
    BgRatio,
    pvalue,
    p.adjust,
    qvalue,
    geneID,
    Count
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p.adjust, pvalue) %>%
  print(n = 30)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Inspect expected heat/stress/chaperone-related terms
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- Expected heat/stress/chaperone-like terms from installed OrgDb enrichGO ---\n")

heatshock_expected_terms_all <- heatshock_results_all %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    stringr::str_detect(
      Description,
      stringr::regex(
        "heat|temperature|stress|chaperone|protein folding|unfolded|HSP",
        ignore_case = TRUE
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p.adjust, pvalue)

print(heatshock_expected_terms_all, n = Inf)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Run ontology-specific tests
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BP is usually the most biologically interpretable.
# MF may return chaperone binding / unfolded protein binding.
# CC may return cellular-component terms.

ego_heatshock_bp <- clusterProfiler::enrichGO(
  gene = heatshock_genes_valid,
  universe = all_orgdb_genes,
  OrgDb = orgdb,
  keyType = "GID",
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 1,
  qvalueCutoff = 1,
  minGSSize = 2,
  maxGSSize = 500,
  readable = FALSE
)

ego_heatshock_mf <- clusterProfiler::enrichGO(
  gene = heatshock_genes_valid,
  universe = all_orgdb_genes,
  OrgDb = orgdb,
  keyType = "GID",
  ont = "MF",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 1,
  qvalueCutoff = 1,
  minGSSize = 2,
  maxGSSize = 500,
  readable = FALSE
)

ego_heatshock_cc <- clusterProfiler::enrichGO(
  gene = heatshock_genes_valid,
  universe = all_orgdb_genes,
  OrgDb = orgdb,
  keyType = "GID",
  ont = "CC",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 1,
  qvalueCutoff = 1,
  minGSSize = 2,
  maxGSSize = 500,
  readable = FALSE
)

heatshock_bp <- as.data.frame(ego_heatshock_bp) %>% tibble::as_tibble()
heatshock_mf <- as.data.frame(ego_heatshock_mf) %>% tibble::as_tibble()
heatshock_cc <- as.data.frame(ego_heatshock_cc) %>% tibble::as_tibble()

cat("\n--- BP top terms ---\n")
heatshock_bp %>%
  dplyr::select(
    ID,
    Description,
    GeneRatio,
    BgRatio,
    pvalue,
    p.adjust,
    qvalue,
    geneID,
    Count
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p.adjust, pvalue) %>%
  print(n = 20)

cat("\n--- MF top terms ---\n")
heatshock_mf %>%
  dplyr::select(
    ID,
    Description,
    GeneRatio,
    BgRatio,
    pvalue,
    p.adjust,
    qvalue,
    geneID,
    Count
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p.adjust, pvalue) %>%
  print(n = 20)

cat("\n--- CC top terms ---\n")
heatshock_cc %>%
  dplyr::select(
    ID,
    Description,
    GeneRatio,
    BgRatio,
    pvalue,
    p.adjust,
    qvalue,
    geneID,
    Count
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p.adjust, pvalue) %>%
  print(n = 20)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Show which heat-shock genes drive the top installed-OrgDb terms
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

if (nrow(heatshock_results_all) > 0) {

  top_heatshock_gene_hits <- heatshock_results_all %>%
    dplyr::arrange(p.adjust, pvalue) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    dplyr::select(ONTOLOGY, ID, Description, geneID) %>%
    tidyr::separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/") %>%
    dplyr::rename(GID = geneID) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(
      orgdb_gene_info %>%
        dplyr::select(GID, SYMBOL, GENENAME) %>%
        dplyr::distinct(),
      by = "GID"
    )

  cat("\n--- Heat-shock genes contributing to top installed-OrgDb enriched terms ---\n")
  print(top_heatshock_gene_hits, n = Inf)
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Optional lightweight negative control using installed OrgDb
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This runs only one random gene set of the same size as the heat-shock set.
# It is just a quick sanity check, not a formal permutation test.

set.seed(123)

random_genes <- sample(
  x = all_orgdb_genes,
  size = length(heatshock_genes_valid),
  replace = FALSE
)

ego_random_all <- clusterProfiler::enrichGO(
  gene = random_genes,
  universe = all_orgdb_genes,
  OrgDb = orgdb,
  keyType = "GID",
  ont = "ALL",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 1,
  qvalueCutoff = 1,
  minGSSize = 2,
  maxGSSize = 500,
  readable = FALSE,
  pool = FALSE
)

random_results_all <- as.data.frame(ego_random_all) %>%
  tibble::as_tibble()

cat("\n--- One random gene-set control using installed OrgDb ---\n")

if (nrow(random_results_all) > 0) {
  random_results_all %>%
    dplyr::select(
      ONTOLOGY,
      ID,
      Description,
      GeneRatio,
      BgRatio,
      pvalue,
      p.adjust,
      qvalue,
      geneID,
      Count
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(p.adjust, pvalue) %>%
    print(n = 20)
} else {
  cat("No enriched GO terms returned for the random gene set.\n")
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Compare heat-shock result to the one random control
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

heatshock_min_padj <- if (nrow(heatshock_results_all) > 0) {
  min(heatshock_results_all$p.adjust, na.rm = TRUE)
} else {
  NA_real_
}

random_min_padj <- if (nrow(random_results_all) > 0) {
  min(random_results_all$p.adjust, na.rm = TRUE)
} else {
  NA_real_
}

quick_control_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  test = "installed_OrgDb_heat_shock_vs_one_random_set",
  n_heatshock_genes_used = length(heatshock_genes_valid),
  heatshock_min_padj = heatshock_min_padj,
  random_min_padj = random_min_padj,
  heatshock_stronger_than_random = heatshock_min_padj < random_min_padj
)

cat("\n--- Quick random-control summary ---\n")
print(quick_control_summary)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. Interpretation guide
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n--- Interpretation guide ---\n")
cat("This test uses clusterProfiler::enrichGO() against the installed org.Spistillata2.eg.db package.\n")
cat("Good sign: heat-shock genes enrich heat, stress, chaperone, protein folding, or unfolded-protein terms.\n")
cat("Good sign: the heat-shock set looks more biologically coherent than one random gene set of the same size.\n")
cat("Caution: genes were selected by GENENAME containing 'heat shock', so this is a database sanity check, not an independent biological discovery.\n")
cat("Caution: genes without GO terms are still valid OrgDb genes, but they cannot contribute to GO enrichment.\n")
```

### 4) Read in functions needed to run GO analyses

```{r}
# Read in gene coordinates from GFF
read_gene_coords <- function(gff_path) {
  gr <- rtracklayer::import(gff_path)
  df <- as_tibble(gr)
  
  gene_coords <- df %>%
    # keep only gene features, or rows with ID that look like "gene-..."
    filter(tolower(type) == "gene" | grepl("^gene-", ID)) %>%
    transmute(
      # remove the "gene-" prefix if present
      gene_id  = sub("^gene-", "", dplyr::coalesce(ID, Name, gene)),
      scaffold = as.character(seqnames),
      start    = as.integer(start),
      end      = as.integer(end),
      strand   = as.character(strand)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(gene_id), !is.na(scaffold), !is.na(start), !is.na(end)) %>%
    distinct(gene_id, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  if (nrow(gene_coords) == 0) {
    stop("No gene coordinates found. Check your GFF 'type' and attribute columns.")
  }
  
  gene_coords
}

# Read in window coordinates from CSV
read_windows <- function(windows_csv) {
  readr::read_csv(
    windows_csv,
    col_types = cols(
      window   = col_character(),
      scaffold = col_character(),
      start    = col_double(),
      end      = col_double()
    )
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      start = as.integer(start),
      end   = as.integer(end)
    ) %>%
    distinct(window, scaffold, start, end)
}
```

### 5) Re-run enrichment with GOs rolled at specific GO level
```{r}
# Load raw mapping 
term2gene_raw <- read_tsv("term2gene_GO.tsv",
                          col_names = c("GO","GID"),
                          skip = 1,
                          show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(GID = sub("-P[0-9]+$", "", GID))

# GO DAG + roots
go_obo <- get_ontology("go.obo",
                       propagate_relationships = "is_a",
                       extract_tags = "everything",
                       merge_equivalent_terms = TRUE)

roots <- c(BP = "GO:0008150", MF = "GO:0003674", CC = "GO:0005575")

# Helpers: namespace, depths, rollup
get_namespace <- function(go_id, ontology, roots) {
  anc <- ontology$ancestors[[go_id]]
  match <- roots[roots %in% anc]
  if (length(match) == 0) return(NA)
  if (length(match) > 1) warning(paste("Multiple roots for", go_id))
  names(match)[1]
}

get_all_depths <- function(go_id, ontology, root, depth = 0) {
  if (go_id == root) return(depth)
  if (length(ontology$parents[[go_id]]) == 0) return(Inf)
  unlist(lapply(ontology$parents[[go_id]], function(p) {
    get_all_depths(p, ontology, root, depth + 1)
  }))
}

get_term_depths <- function(go_id, ontology, roots) {
  ns <- get_namespace(go_id, ontology, roots)
  if (is.na(ns)) return(list(Namespace = NA, Depth_min = NA, Depth_max = NA))
  root <- roots[[ns]]
  depths <- get_all_depths(go_id, ontology, root)
  list(Namespace = ns,
       Depth_min = min(depths, na.rm = TRUE),
       Depth_max = max(depths, na.rm = TRUE))
}

go_lookup <- map_dfr(unique(term2gene_raw$GO), function(go) {
  out <- get_term_depths(go, go_obo, roots)
  tibble(GO = go,
         Namespace = out$Namespace,
         Depth_min = out$Depth_min,
         Depth_max = out$Depth_max)
})

term2gene_with_levels <- term2gene_raw %>%
  left_join(go_lookup, by = "GO")

get_anc_at_depth <- function(go_id, ontology, root, target_depth, current_depth = 0) {
  if (go_id == root && current_depth == target_depth) return(go_id)
  if (current_depth == target_depth) return(go_id)
  if (length(ontology$parents[[go_id]]) == 0) return(NA)
  unlist(lapply(ontology$parents[[go_id]], function(p) {
    get_anc_at_depth(p, ontology, root, target_depth, current_depth + 1)
  }))
}

rollup_to_level <- function(df, ontology, roots, target_depth) {
  df %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      Rolled_GO = if (Depth_min <= target_depth) {
        GO
      } else {
        root <- roots[[Namespace]]
        paste(unique(get_anc_at_depth(GO, ontology, root, target_depth)), collapse = ";")
      }
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    separate_rows(Rolled_GO, sep = ";") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Rolled_GO) & Rolled_GO != "")
}

# Choose your target level once (adjust as you like) 
target_depth <- 6
rolled <- rollup_to_level(term2gene_with_levels, go_obo, roots, target_depth)

term2gene_rolled <- rolled %>%
  dplyr::select(GO = Rolled_GO, GID) %>%
  distinct()

# Function for rolled-level enrichment
analyze_population_rolled <- function(file, pop_name,
                                      gene_coords,
                                      windows_tbl,
                                      term2gene_rolled,
                                      top_fraction = 0.05,
                                      node_size = 1,
                                      ontology = "BP",
                                      orgdb = "org.Spistillata.eg.db",
                                      semdata) {
  message("Processing ROLLED: ", pop_name)
  
  wza_out <- read.csv(file)
  
  t2g <- term2gene_rolled %>% distinct(GO, GID)
  gene2GO <- split(t2g$GO, t2g$GID)
  all_genes <- sort(unique(t2g$GID))
  
  # filter to the top `top_fraction` of windows by Z_pVal (per population)
  pval_cutoff <- quantile(wza_out$Z_pVal, probs = top_fraction, na.rm = TRUE)
  windows_within_threshold <- wza_out %>% filter(Z_pVal <= pval_cutoff)
  coordinates_of_windows <- windows_tbl %>% filter(window %in% windows_within_threshold$gene)
  
  if (nrow(coordinates_of_windows) == 0) {
    return(list(population = pop_name, allRes = tibble(), reducedTerms = NULL,
                simMatrix = NULL, pval_cutoff = pval_cutoff))
  }
  
  # GRanges
  gr_windows <- makeGRangesFromDataFrame(coordinates_of_windows,
                                         seqnames.field = "scaffold",
                                         start.field = "start",
                                         end.field = "end",
                                         keep.extra.columns = TRUE)
  
  gr_genes <- makeGRangesFromDataFrame(gene_coords,
                                       seqnames.field = "scaffold",
                                       start.field = "start",
                                       end.field = "end",
                                       keep.extra.columns = TRUE)
  
  # harmonize seqlevels
  common_seqlevels <- intersect(seqlevels(gr_windows), seqlevels(gr_genes))
  gr_windows <- keepSeqlevels(gr_windows, common_seqlevels, pruning.mode = "coarse")
  gr_genes   <- keepSeqlevels(gr_genes, common_seqlevels, pruning.mode = "coarse")
  
  hits <- findOverlaps(gr_windows, gr_genes)

  overlap_tbl <- tibble(
    window  = mcols(gr_windows)$window[queryHits(hits)],
    gene_id = mcols(gr_genes)$gene_id[subjectHits(hits)])
  
  all_sig_genes <- unique(overlap_tbl$gene_id)
  
  geneList <- factor(as.integer(all_genes %in% all_sig_genes))
  names(geneList) <- all_genes
  
  if (sum(geneList == 1) == 0) {
    return(list(population = pop_name, allRes = tibble(), reducedTerms = NULL,
                simMatrix = NULL, pval_cutoff = pval_cutoff))
  }
  
  GOdata <- new("topGOdata",
                ontology = ontology,
                allGenes = geneList,
                annot = annFUN.gene2GO,
                gene2GO = gene2GO,
                nodeSize = node_size)
  
  res_classic_Fisher <- runTest(GOdata, algorithm = "classic", statistic = "fisher")
  res_elim_Fisher    <- runTest(GOdata, algorithm = "elim", statistic = "fisher")
  res_weight_Fisher  <- runTest(GOdata, algorithm = "weight01", statistic = "fisher")
  
  allRes <- GenTable(GOdata,
                     classic_Fisher = res_classic_Fisher,
                     elim_Fisher    = res_elim_Fisher,
                     weight_Fisher  = res_weight_Fisher,
                     orderBy        = "classic_Fisher",
                     topNodes       = 500) %>%
    mutate(across(c(classic_Fisher, elim_Fisher, weight_Fisher), as.numeric)) %>%
    filter(classic_Fisher < 0.05 | elim_Fisher < 0.05 | weight_Fisher < 0.05)
  
  if (nrow(allRes) == 0) {
    return(list(population = pop_name, allRes = tibble(), reducedTerms = NULL,
                simMatrix = NULL, pval_cutoff = pval_cutoff))
  }
  
  scores <- -log10(allRes$elim_Fisher)
  names(scores) <- allRes$GO.ID
  
  simMatrix <- calculateSimMatrix(allRes$GO.ID,
                                  orgdb = orgdb,
                                  ont = ontology,
                                  method = "Rel",
                                  semdata = semdata)
  
  reducedTerms <- reduceSimMatrix(simMatrix, scores,
                                  threshold = 0.7,
                                  orgdb = orgdb,
                                  keytype = "GID")
  
  return(list(population = pop_name, allRes = allRes, reducedTerms = reducedTerms,
              simMatrix = simMatrix, pval_cutoff = pval_cutoff))
}

# Run rolled-level enrichment
files <- list(
  Global  = "wza_output_Global.csv",
  Moore   = "wza_output_Moore.csv",
  Lizard  = "wza_output_Lizard.csv",
  Heron   = "wza_output_Heron.csv",
  Pelorus = "wza_output_Pelorus.csv",
  Central = "wza_output_CentralOffshore.csv")

windows_tbl <- read.csv("all_windows_coords.csv")
gene_coords <- read_gene_coords(gff_file)

# Run rolled-level enrichment
semdata <- godata(orgdb, keytype = "GID", ont = "BP")

rolled_outputs <- lapply(names(files), function(pop) {
  analyze_population_rolled(file = files[[pop]],
                            pop_name = pop,
                            gene_coords = gene_coords,
                            windows_tbl = windows_tbl,
                            term2gene_rolled = term2gene_rolled,
                            ontology = "BP",
                            orgdb = orgdb,
                            semdata = semdata)
})

names(rolled_outputs) <- names(files)
saveRDS(rolled_outputs, "rolled_outputs_level6.rds")
```

#### # 3E) Plot results of GO enrichment at specific level
```{r}
# Comparing across populations
library(UpSetR)

all_summaries <- bind_rows(lapply(names(rolled_outputs), function(pop) {
  df <- rolled_outputs[[pop]]$reducedTerms
  if (!is.null(df)) {
    rownames(df) <- NULL   # remove row names
    df$Pop <- pop
    df
  }
}))

all_summaries <- all_summaries %>% filter(!is.na(parent))  # guard in case some reducedTerms were NULL

all_summaries_more_than_1 <- all_summaries %>%
  group_by(parent) %>%
  mutate(distinct_pops = n_distinct(Pop)) %>%
  arrange(distinct_pops) %>%
  filter(distinct_pops >= 2)

parent_pop <- all_summaries %>%
  dplyr::select(parent, Pop) %>%
  distinct()

parent_matrix <- parent_pop %>%
  mutate(value = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Pop, values_from = value, values_fill = 0)

upset(as.data.frame(parent_matrix),
      sets = c("Heron", "Pelorus", "Central", "Moore", "Lizard", "Global"),
      nintersects = NA,
      keep.order = TRUE,
      order.by = "freq")

# Strongest signals within each population - barplots

top_terms_per_pop <- bind_rows(lapply(names(rolled_outputs), function(pop) {
  df <- rolled_outputs[[pop]]$allRes
  if (!is.null(df) && nrow(df) > 0) {
    df <- df %>%
      arrange(elim_Fisher) %>%
      slice_head(n = 10) %>%
      mutate(Pop = pop)
    df
  }
}))

top_terms_per_pop <- top_terms_per_pop %>%
  mutate(Term = fct_reorder(Term, -elim_Fisher))

ordered_pops <- c("Global", "Lizard", "Moore", "Central", "Pelorus", "Heron")
pop_colors <- c("grey40", "#B05102", "#F39237", "#1F7D1E", "#F2C738", "#5CBED1")

ggplot(top_terms_per_pop, aes(x = Term, y = -log10(elim_Fisher), fill = Pop)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~Pop, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(pop_colors, ordered_pops)) +
  coord_flip() +
  ylab("-log10(elim_Fisher)") +
  xlab("GO term") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))

# Strongest signals within each population - heatmap

top_terms_per_pop <- bind_rows(lapply(names(rolled_outputs), function(pop) {
  df <- rolled_outputs[[pop]]$allRes
  if (!is.null(df) && nrow(df) > 0) {
    df %>%
      arrange(elim_Fisher) %>%
      slice_head(n = 5) %>%
      mutate(Pop = pop)
  }
}))

terms_of_interest <- unique(top_terms_per_pop$Term)

heatmap_df <- lapply(names(rolled_outputs), function(pop) {
  df <- rolled_outputs[[pop]]$allRes
  if (!is.null(df) && nrow(df) > 0) {
    df %>%
      filter(Term %in% terms_of_interest) %>%
      dplyr::select(Term, elim_Fisher) %>%
      mutate(Pop = pop)
  }
}) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  tidyr::complete(Term = terms_of_interest, Pop = ordered_pops) %>%
  mutate(logp = -log10(elim_Fisher))

heatmap_wide <- heatmap_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Pop, Term, logp) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Pop, values_from = logp, values_fill = 0)  # fill missing with 0

term_order <- heatmap_wide %>%
  mutate(sig_pattern = paste0(ifelse(Heron>0,1,0),
                              ifelse(Pelorus>0,1,0),
                              ifelse(Central>0,1,0),
                              ifelse(Moore>0,1,0),
                              ifelse(Lizard>0,1,0),
                              ifelse(Global>0,1,0))) %>%
  arrange(sig_pattern, desc(Heron + Pelorus + Central + Moore + Lizard + Global)) %>%
  pull(Term)

heatmap_long <- heatmap_df %>%
  mutate(Term = factor(Term, levels = rev(term_order)),
         Pop = factor(Pop, levels = ordered_pops))

ggplot(heatmap_long, aes(x = Pop, y = Term, fill = logp)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = c("steelblue", "darkred"),
                       na.value = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```
